title: The Crew Drifts Toward a Strange Island
description: On pages 14–17, Luffy, Zoro, and Nami reach a strange island where Gaimon and the rare animals guard a lonely mystery.
scene: The crew’s lighthearted exploration turns into a small fable about treasure, isolation, and the life Gaimon built after being trapped on the island.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That means we need to sail around the world!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Idiots! That place is not that easy to find!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It also has another name... "The Pirate's graveyard"....

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Long ago, I saw Pirates...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...that returned from the "Grand Line"...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Everyone of them had lost their spirit and looked as if they were dead

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Their facial expressions were even more painful to look at...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It looked like they had met some- thing horrid,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Maybe a dreadful pirate or perhaps a monster

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Nobody dared to talk about it...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You could see from their look,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The horror of the "Grand Line"!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I have no other proof than this,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The rumors only made it more and more myste- rious!

dialogue.speaker: Gaimon
dialogue.text: It's already been more than 20 years since the beginning of the great pirate era...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: that treasure might be nothing more than legend

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Can you see now? "One piece" is only a dream inside a dream

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Is that so?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I think that there is a better ending, right?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If a person really found such a treasure, he wouldn't tell anybody!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That is what a smart person would do!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I will surely find the treasure, because I'm always lucky!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I just can't under- stand where you get such con-idence.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Now I will tell you, why I can't leave this island...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: OH! WHY IS THAT?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's because I'm not mature!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I don't know how to adapt to the changes in situations!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Gaimon
dialogue.text: It hap- pened 20 years ago,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I was a pirate and the ship docked on this island...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: because we found a map that said...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...that there was treasure on this island...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We don't have to look for it anymore!

summary: This part works as a pause between major arcs, showing that the sea contains strange lives and unusual dreams beyond simple battles.
